reasoning: According to multiple reliable sources, including BBC News as of April 2025 and an official declaration of the provisional electoral list on March 17, 2025, former President Laurent Gbagbo is legally barred from running in the 2025 presidential election and remains excluded from the electoral roll. This explicitly fulfills the resolution criteria, which states the question resolves as Yes if "any of these candidates has been removed from the electoral list or otherwise become legally ineligible to run in the 2025 presidential election... before July 1, 2025." There is a small margin of doubt (the 4% difference from certainty) regarding whether an amnesty or legal reversal could theoretically be enacted before the July 1, 2025 cutoff to un-disqualify him or challenge the semantic interpretation that he "has been removed" versus "was already ineligible and remains so," but the fact that the actual exclusion occurred with the March 2025 provisional list publication makes the condition definitively met.
